Knowledge-Cutoff Benchmark · Explorer

What do frontier models actually know — and when did they stop knowing it?

Each model was probed on 198 surprising, unforecastable events across 18 months. Below: where each model's knowledge collapses (its effective cutoff), a month-by-month heatmap, and — for every single event — the exact answer each model gave. Pick an event and read them yourself.

Decay curves · % correct per month click a model to toggle · hover for values
Heatmap · correctness by month click any cell to inspect those answers below
doesn't know knows  ·  shade = % correct that month
Event explorer · read the actual answers
direct = open question graded correct / wrong / abstain by an LLM judge holding the ground truth · mcq = 4-way forced choice (chance ≈ 25%)
effective cutoff = last month above 50% correct that stays gone afterward · controls: living people (should be "alive") + fabricated events (should be denied)
198 events · Jan 2025 – Jun 2026 · surprising / unforecastable only · balanced US / international
